he: פסוקי דזמרה
en: Pesukei DZimrah

Before we begin Pesukei D'Zimrah with its opening brachah of Baruch She'amar, we say this chapter of Tehillim that David HaMelech created for the eventual Chanukas HaBayis—inauguration of the Beis Hamikdash—which interestingly enough, he was not allowed to build!

How is this relevant to us today, and what does it have to do with Pesukei D'Zimrah and the Beis Hamikdash?

This chapter discusses the difficulties (physical, emotional, and spiritual) that David HaMelech faced in his lifetime, and his belief that Hashem could and would pull him through it all. He also expresses his belief that eventually his labors would bear fruit (the Beis Hamikdash would be built), even though he knew it was ordained by Hashem that he would not live to see it! Yet he did the best he could with the struggles Hashem sent his way and thanked Hashem for all he did accomplish. We too have no idea whether Hashem will allow us to see the end results of all our hard work! Yet we will now say Pesukei D'Zimrah and thank Hashem for all He has done and proclaim our belief that He controls all the circumstances in our lives. We will say the Shema and proclaim our loyalty to His Torah through thick and thin, and based on that, we will ask Him to give us the things we think we need in order to accomplish our mission (Shemoneh Esrei).

And we do this with joy and faith even though we do not know whether we will merit to see it all to fruition or not!

Importantly, the Malbim says that this chapter really refers to our body—also a mini Beis Hamikdash—a physical thing that we fill with kedushah and use to fulfill our mission. And, like the real Beis Hamikdash, it must be treated with respect and sanctity!

Some find Pesukei D'Zimrah "annoying"—all this praising of Hashem strikes them as strange. "What does He need that for?" Of course, Hashem doesn't need our praises—*we* need to praise *Him*! For an explanation of this crucial idea, see the Introduction to Birkas Ha'mazon on page 119.

Much of Pesukei D'Zimrah is made up of Tehillim, which is poetry. The nuances and beauty of the various paragraphs are too easily lost, which is why some people have the unfortunate "custom" of saying just Baruch She'amar, Ashrei, and Yishtabach. Here, we will try to convey the point of each part, but this can never do justice to the depth, beauty, and lofty spiritual ideas David HaMelech wove into every sentence. Still, any improvement counts!
Introduction to Pesukei D'Zimrah

he: מִזְמוֹר שִׁיר חֲנֻכַּת הַבַּיִת
en: Mizmor Shir Chanukas HaBayis

he: מִזְמוֹר שִׁיר חֲנֻכַּת הַבַּיִת לְדָוִד: אֲרוֹמִמְךָ יְהֹוָה כִּי דִלִּיתָנִי, וְלֹא שִׂמַּחְתָּ אֹיְבַי לִי: יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהָי, שִׁוַּעְתִּי אֵלֶיךָ וַתִּרְפָּאֵנִי: יְהֹוָה הֶעֱלִיתָ מִן שְׁאוֹל נַפְשִׁי, חִיִּיתַנִי מִיָּרְדִי בוֹר: זַמְּרוּ לַיהֹוָה חֲסִידָיו, וְהוֹדוּ לְזֵכֶר קָדְשׁוֹ: כִּי רֶגַע בְּאַפּוֹ, חַיִּים בִּרְצוֹנוֹ, בָּעֶרֶב יָלִין בֶּכִי וְלַבֹּקֶר רִנָּה: וַאֲנִי אָמַרְתִּי בְשַׁלְוִי, בַּל אֶמּוֹט לְעוֹלָם: יְהֹוָה בִּרְצוֹנְךָ הֶעֱמַדְתָּה לְהַרְרִי עֹז, הִסְתַּרְתָּ פָנֶיךָ הָיִיתִי נִבְהָל: אֵלֶיךָ יְהֹוָה אֶקְרָא, וְאֶל אֲדֹנָי אֶתְחַנָּן: מַה בֶּצַע בְּדָמִי, בְּרִדְתִּי אֶל שָׁחַת, הֲיוֹדְךָ עָפָר, הֲיַגִּיד אֲמִתֶּךָ: שְׁמַע יְהֹוָה וְחָנֵּנִי, יְהֹוָה הֱיֵה עֹזֵר לִי: הָפַכְתָּ מִסְפְּדִי לְמָחוֹל לִי, פִּתַּחְתָּ שַׂקִּי וַתְּאַזְּרֵנִי שִׂמְחָה: לְמַעַן יְזַמֶּרְךָ כָבוֹד וְלֹא יִדֹּם, יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהַי לְעוֹלָם אוֹדֶךָּ:

en: —*מִזְמוֹר שִׁיר חֲנֻכַּת הַבַּיִת* A song composed by David HaMelech to be used in the eventual dedication of the Beis Hamikdash—I praise You because You have raised me up from the depths of all my difficulties—and You did not let my enemies (*real or imagined*) triumph over me. I called out to You and You healed me; You brought me back up when I was down. I should always be singing Your praises because Your "anger" is only for a moment—and its purpose is really to improve our lives! Though *בְּרֶגַע*—in "dark times" (*when we are in pain*) we do not understand the purpose of our pain, we are confident that *לַבֹּקֶר*—"in the morning" (*when all is clear*), we will be joyful. *וַאֲנִי אָמַרְתִּי בְשַׁלְוִי*—When all is calm and my body healthy, I think *בַּל אֶמּוֹט לְעוֹלָם*—that state is natural and will last forever. But I know that it is You, Hashem, Who keeps me strong. If You would chas v'shalom "turn away from me"—all would fall apart. Therefore, I turn to You for help; it is to You I call out and proclaim: "My goal is to accomplish the mission You gave me." Therefore, I need Your (*material and emotional*) support to allow me to do that! *הָפַכְתָּ מִסְפְּדִי לְמָחוֹל לִי*—So many times You turn my difficulties into growth opportunities (*even though I do not always realize that at the moment*)—all in order for me to accomplish my mission. Hashem Who takes care of me—in all circumstances I will praise You!

The first letters of the first four words of this *מִזְמוֹר שִׁיר חֲנֻכַּת* (which discusses all of David HaMelech's difficulties) spell out *שמח*. True, deep simchah is based on rock-solid belief that in the end all is for the best and a Beis Hamikdash will result! Even though we cannot always see the sense of Hashem's plan, hinted to by the fact that the letters are not in order.

he: קדיש יתום
en: Kaddish Yasom

he: יִתְגַּדַּל וְיִתְקַדַּשׁ שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא, בְּעָלְמָא דִּי בְרָא כִרְעוּתֵהּ וְיַמְלִיךְ מַלְכוּתֵהּ בְּחַיֵּיכוֹן וּבְיוֹמֵיכוֹן וּבְחַיֵּי דְכָל בֵּית יִשְׂרָאֵל, בַּעֲגָלָא וּבִזְמַן קָרִיב וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן: יְהֵא שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא מְבָרַךְ לְעָלַם וּלְעָלְמֵי עָלְמַיָּא: יִתְבָּרַךְ וְיִשְׁתַּבַּח, וְיִתְפָּאַר וְיִתְרוֹמַם, וְיִתְנַשֵּׂא וְיִתְהַדָּר, וְיִתְעַלֶּה וְיִתְהַלָּל, שְׁמֵהּ דְּקֻדְשָׁא בְּרִיךְ הוּא: לְעֵלָּא מִן כָּל בִּרְכָתָא (בעשי"ת לְעֵלָּא וּלְעֵלָּא מִכָּל בִּרְכָתָא) וְשִׁירָתָא, תֻּשְׁבְּחָתָא וְנֶחֱמָתָא, דַּאֲמִירָן בְּעָלְמָא, וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן: יְהֵא שְׁלָמָא רַבָּא מִן שְׁמַיָּא וְחַיִּים עָלֵינוּ וְעַל כָּל יִשְׂרָאֵל, וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן: עֹשֶׂה שָׁלוֹם (בעשי"ת הַשָּׁלוֹם) בִּמְרוֹמָיו, הוּא יַעֲשֶׂה שָׁלוֹם עָלֵינוּ וְעַל כָּל יִשְׂרָאֵל וְאִמְרוּ אָמֵן:

en: *יִתְגַּדַּל וְיִתְקַדַּשׁ שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא* (*The person saying Kaddish proclaims our hope that*) soon, in our lifetime, the entire world, which Hashem created precisely according to His Will, will recognize Hashem as the Source of all and will see the sanctity, perfection, and purpose of all He has done. *אָמֵן. יְהֵא שְׁמֵהּ רַבָּא מְבָרַךְ לְעָלַם וּלְעָלְמֵי עָלְמַיָּא* (*We all enthusiastically respond:*) Absolutely correct! May everyone (*including me!*), everywhere, and forever, see that all His actions are great and deserving of praise! *יִתְבָּרַךְ וְיִשְׁתַּבַּח* (*The person saying Kaddish then points out that*) in reality, the true greatness and power of Hashem far exceeds any praises that we mere humans could say. And to that we respond: *אָמֵן*—Absolutely correct! *יְהֵא שְׁלָמָא רַבָּא* (*The person saying Kaddish requests:*) May there be abundant peace, that comes from Heaven, and life, upon us and upon all of Bnei Yisrael. And to that we respond: *אָמֵן*—Absolutely, we agree! *עֹשֶׂה שָׁלוֹם בִּמְרוֹמָיו* (*The person saying Kaddish requests:*) May He Who makes peace in the Heavens bring about peace upon us and upon all of Bnei Yisrael! And to that we respond: *אָמֵן*—Absolutely, we agree!
Opening Brachah of Pesukei D'Zimrah

he: בָּרוּךְ שֶׁאָמַר
en: Baruch She'amar

he: הָרֵינִי מְזַמֵּן אֶת פִּי לְהוֹדוֹת וּלְהַלֵּל וּלְשַׁבֵּחַ אֶת בּוֹרְאִי לְשֵׁם יִחוּד קוּדְשָׁא בְּרִיךְ הוּא וּשְׁכִינְתֵּהּ עַל יְדֵי הַהוּא טָמִיר וְנֶעְלָם בְּשֵׁם כָּל יִשְׂרָאֵל.

en: FAQ: But how am I supposed to even sing all these praises to Hashem when my life is so difficult? I have so much stuff going on, and it's hard to accept that all is fair and just! ANSWER: That is the classic question of "tzaddik v'ra lo—why the righteous suffer"! See Appendix 2.

he: בָּרוּךְ שֶׁאָמַר וְהָיָה הָעוֹלָם, בָּרוּךְ הוּא: בָּרוּךְ עוֹשֶׂה בְרֵאשִׁית, בָּרוּךְ אוֹמֵר וְעוֹשֶׂה, בָּרוּךְ גּוֹזֵר וּמְקַיֵּם, בָּרוּךְ מְרַחֵם עַל הָאָרֶץ, בָּרוּךְ מְרַחֵם עַל הַבְּרִיּוֹת, בָּרוּךְ מְשַׁלֵּם שָׂכָר טוֹב לִירֵאָיו, בָּרוּךְ חַי לָעַד וְקַיָּם לָנֶצַח, בָּרוּךְ פּוֹדֶה וּמַצִּיל בָּרוּךְ שְׁמוֹ: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, הָאֵל הָאָב הָרַחֲמָן הַמְהֻלָּל בְּפֶה עַמּוֹ, מְשֻׁבָּח וּמְפֹאָר בִּלְשׁוֹן חֲסִידָיו וַעֲבָדָיו, וּבְשִׁירֵי דָוִד עַבְדֶּךָ נְהַלֶּלְךָ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ בִּשְׁבָחוֹת וּבִזְמִירוֹת, נְגַדֶּלְךָ וּנְשַׁבֵּחֲךָ וּנְפָאֶרְךָ וְנַזְכִּיר שִׁמְךָ וְנַמְלִיכְךָ מַלְכֵּנוּ אֱלֹהֵינוּ: יָחִיד חֵי הָעוֹלָמִים, מֶלֶךְ מְשֻׁבָּח וּמְפֹאָר עֲדֵי עַד שְׁמוֹ הַגָּדוֹל: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה מֶלֶךְ מְהֻלָּל בַּתִּשְׁבָּחוֹת:

en: *בָּרוּךְ שֶׁאָמַר*—*בָּרוּךְ* The Source of everything (*Hashem*) spoke and created the entire universe; He makes decrees and fulfills them; He shows mercy on all His creatures (*giving them opportunities to correct their mistakes*); He rewards those who fear Him; and He is Eternal (*there is Divine justice, even though as limited humans we cannot always see it*); He redeems and rescues us (*personally from our own physical and mental traumas; and as a nation throughout our history*); to sum it all up—*בָּרוּךְ שְׁמוֹ*—His Presence is the Source for everything! *בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה*—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; *אֱלֹהֵינוּ*—You take care of us; *מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם*—and You rule the entire world; the All-powerful (*yet at the same time*) merciful Father Who is praised by His entire nation. With the Tehillim of David HaMelech Your servant, we too will now (*in the upcoming sections of Pesukei D'Zimrah*) sing Your praises and affirm Your rulership over us. *יָחִיד חֵי הָעוֹלָמִים*—You are the Sole Giver of life to the entire universe; the King Whose actions are praised eternally (*for we believe that they are all fair and just*). *בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה*—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; *מֶלֶךְ מְהֻלָּל בַּתִּשְׁבָּחוֹת*—the King Who is praised by all (*including me!*).
Opening Paragraph of Pesukei D'Zimrah

he: הוֹדוּ לַיהֹוָה קִרְאוּ בִשְׁמוֹ
en: Hodu LaShem Kir'u Vishmo
Since Hodu is very long and a collection of various pesukim and statements (rather than being one chapter of Tehillim), we are going to break it up into sections and focus on a few key phrases.

he: הוֹדוּ לַיהֹוָה קִרְאוּ בִשְׁמוֹ, הוֹדִיעוּ בָעַמִּים עֲלִילוֹתָיו: שִׁירוּ לוֹ זַמְּרוּ לוֹ, שִׂיחוּ בְּכָל נִפְלְאוֹתָיו: הִתְהַלְלוּ בְּשֵׁם קָדְשׁוֹ, יִשְׂמַח לֵב מְבַקְשֵׁי יְהֹוָה: דִּרְשׁוּ יְהֹוָה וְעֻזּוֹ, בַּקְּשׁוּ פָנָיו תָּמִיד: זִכְרוּ נִפְלְאוֹתָיו אֲשֶׁר עָשָׂה, מֹפְתָיו וּמִשְׁפְּטֵי פִיהוּ: זֶרַע יִשְׂרָאֵל עַבְדּוֹ, בְּנֵי יַעֲקֹב בְּחִירָיו: הוּא יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ, בְּכָל הָאָרֶץ מִשְׁפָּטָיו: זִכְרוּ לְעוֹלָם בְּרִיתוֹ, דָּבָר צִוָּה לְאֶלֶף דּוֹר: אֲשֶׁר כָּרַת אֶת אַבְרָהָם, וּשְׁבוּעָתוֹ לְיִצְחָק: וַיַּעֲמִידֶהָ לְיַעֲקֹב לְחֹק, לְיִשְׂרָאֵל בְּרִית עוֹלָם: לֵאמֹר, לְךָ אֶתֵּן אֶרֶץ כְּנָעַן, חֶבֶל נַחֲלַתְכֶם: בִּהְיוֹתְכֶם מְתֵי מִסְפָּר, כִּמְעַט וְגָרִים בָּהּ: וַיִּתְהַלְּכוּ מִגּוֹי אֶל גּוֹי, וּמִמַּמְלָכָה אֶל עַם אַחֵר: לֹא הִנִּיחַ לְאִישׁ לְעָשְׁקָם, וַיּוֹכַח עֲלֵיהֶם מְלָכִים: אַל תִּגְּעוּ בִּמְשִׁיחָי, וּבִנְבִיאַי אַל תָּרֵעוּ: שִׁירוּ לַיהֹוָה כָּל הָאָרֶץ, בַּשְּׂרוּ מִיּוֹם אֶל יוֹם יְשׁוּעָתוֹ: סַפְּרוּ בַגּוֹיִם אֶת כְּבוֹדוֹ, בְּכָל הָעַמִּים נִפְלְאוֹתָיו: כִּי גָדוֹל יְהֹוָה וּמְהֻלָּל מְאֹד, וְנוֹרָא הוּא עַל כָּל אֱלֹהִים: כִּי כָּל אֱלֹהֵי הָעַמִּים אֱלִילִים, וַיהֹוָה שָׁמַיִם עָשָׂה: הוֹד וְהָדָר לְפָנָיו, עֹז וְחֶדְוָה בִּמְקוֹמוֹ: הָבוּ לַיהֹוָה מִשְׁפְּחוֹת עַמִּים, הָבוּ לַיהֹוָה כָּבוֹד וָעֹז: הָבוּ לַיהֹוָה כְּבוֹד שְׁמוֹ, שְׂאוּ מִנְחָה וּבֹאוּ לְפָנָיו, הִשְׁתַּחֲווּ לַיהֹוָה בְּהַדְרַת קֹדֶשׁ: חִילוּ מִלְּפָנָיו כָּל הָאָרֶץ, אַף תִּכּוֹן תֵּבֵל בַּל תִּמּוֹט: יִשְׂמְחוּ הַשָּׁמַיִם וְתָגֵל הָאָרֶץ, וְיֹאמְרוּ בַגּוֹיִם יְהֹוָה מָלָךְ: יִרְעַם הַיָּם וּמְלֹאוֹ, יַעֲלֹץ הַשָּׂדֶה וְכָל אֲשֶׁר בּוֹ: אָז יְרַנְּנוּ עֲצֵי הַיָּעַר, מִלִּפְנֵי יְהֹוָה כִּי בָא לִשְׁפּוֹט אֶת הָאָרֶץ:

en: *הוֹדוּ*—Even though I am now going to personally praise Hashem for all He does, and focus on His power in nature and history (*which will lead me to personally accept His sovereignty on praying—the Shema*), really the entire world should acknowledge the power of Hashem and praise Him. Hashem chose our Avos and Imahos, turned them into a great nation and miraculously gave us Eretz Yisrael (*and we are the center of world attention*). No power throughout history has been able to destroy us. (*Our very centrality on the world stage is itself the greatest proof of Hashem's power and our special bond with Him.*) For that we praise Hashem, and it provides us with a sense of security and joy. *שִׁירוּ לַיהֹוָה ... בַּשְּׂרוּ מִיּוֹם אֶל יוֹם יְשׁוּעָתוֹ*—We await the day (*when Mashiach comes*) and the entire world will acknowledge the truth, and see the power of Hashem in history and in the beauty and majesty of nature. And the entire Creation will proclaim:
Hodu (continued)

he: הוֹדוּ לַיהֹוָה כִּי טוֹב, כִּי לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ: וְאִמְרוּ הוֹשִׁיעֵנוּ אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׁעֵנוּ, וְקַבְּצֵנוּ וְהַצִּילֵנוּ מִן הַגּוֹיִם, לְהֹדוֹת לְשֵׁם קָדְשֶׁךָ, לְהִשְׁתַּבֵּחַ בִּתְהִלָּתֶךָ: בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל מִן הָעוֹלָם וְעַד הָעוֹלָם, וַיֹּאמְרוּ כָל הָעָם אָמֵן וְהַלֵּל לַיהֹוָה: רוֹמְמוּ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ, וְהִשְׁתַּחֲווּ לַהֲדֹם רַגְלָיו, קָדוֹשׁ הוּא: רוֹמְמוּ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ וְהִשְׁתַּחֲווּ לְהַר קָדְשׁוֹ, כִּי קָדוֹשׁ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ: וְהוּא רַחוּם יְכַפֵּר עָוֹן וְלֹא יַשְׁחִית, וְהִרְבָּה לְהָשִׁיב אַפּוֹ, וְלֹא יָעִיר כָּל חֲמָתוֹ: אַתָּה יְהֹוָה לֹא תִכְלָא רַחֲמֶיךָ מִמֶּנִּי, חַסְדְּךָ וַאֲמִתְּךָ תָּמִיד יִצְּרוּנִי: זְכֹר רַחֲמֶיךָ יְהֹוָה וַחֲסָדֶיךָ, כִּי מֵעוֹלָם הֵמָּה: תְּנוּ עֹז לֵאלֹהִים, עַל יִשְׂרָאֵל גַּאֲוָתוֹ, וְעֻזּוֹ בַּשְּׁחָקִים: נוֹרָא אֱלֹהִים מִמִּקְדָּשֶׁיךָ, אֵל יִשְׂרָאֵל הוּא נֹתֵן עֹז וְתַעֲצֻמוֹת לָעָם, בָּרוּךְ אֱלֹהִים: אֵל נְקָמוֹת יְהֹוָה, אֵל נְקָמוֹת הוֹפִיעַ: הִנָּשֵׂא שֹׁפֵט הָאָרֶץ, הָשֵׁב גְּמוּל עַל גֵּאִים: לַיהֹוָה הַיְשׁוּעָה, עַל עַמְּךָ בִרְכָתֶךָ סֶּלָה: יְהֹוָה צְבָאוֹת עִמָּנוּ, מִשְׂגָּב לָנוּ אֱלֹהֵי יַעֲקֹב סֶלָה: יְהֹוָה צְבָאוֹת, אַשְׁרֵי אָדָם בֹּטֵחַ בָּךְ: יְהֹוָה הוֹשִׁיעָה, הַמֶּלֶךְ יַעֲנֵנוּ בְיוֹם קָרְאֵנוּ: הוֹשִׁיעָה אֶת עַמֶּךָ וּבָרֵךְ אֶת נַחֲלָתֶךָ, וּרְעֵם וְנַשְּׂאֵם עַד הָעוֹלָם: נַפְשֵׁנוּ חִכְּתָה לַיהֹוָה, עֶזְרֵנוּ וּמָגִנֵּנוּ הוּא: כִּי בוֹ יִשְׂמַח לִבֵּנוּ, כִּי בְשֵׁם קָדְשׁוֹ בָטָחְנוּ: יְהִי חַסְדְּךָ יְהֹוָה עָלֵינוּ, כַּאֲשֶׁר יִחַלְנוּ לָךְ: הַרְאֵנוּ יְהֹוָה חַסְדֶּךָ, וְיֶשְׁעֲךָ תִּתֶּן לָנוּ: קוּמָה עֶזְרָתָה לָּנוּ, וּפְדֵנוּ לְמַעַן חַסְדֶּךָ: אָנֹכִי יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ הַמַּעַלְךָ מֵאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם, הַרְחֶב פִּיךָ וַאֲמַלְאֵהוּ: אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁכָּכָה לּוֹ, אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁיֲהֹוָה אֱלֹהָיו: וַאֲנִי בְּחַסְדְּךָ בָטַחְתִּי, יָגֵל לִבִּי בִּישׁוּעָתֶךָ, אָשִׁירָה לַיהֹוָה כִּי גָמַל עָלָי:

en: *הוֹדוּ לַיהֹוָה כִּי טוֹב*—Praise and thank Hashem, for ALL that He does is good (*even though now it is hard for us to see that*); *כִּי לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ*—His kindness and care for us is eternal. *וְהִשְׁתַּחֲווּ לַהֲדֹם רַגְלָיו ... קָדוֹשׁ*—Redeem us from our (*physical and spiritual*) galus so that we may serve You in the Beis Hamikdash; forgive us our sins; and raise our esteem among the nations. We place our complete faith and trust in You that You will save us from all misfortunes and give us the ability to fulfill our mission to the world. Just as You took us out of Egypt (*and chose us as Your nation*), we are confident that You will accomplish all our prayers. *אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁכָּכָה לּוֹ*—Happy and fortunate are we, the nation (*Bnei Yisrael*) that has all this glorious history and great mission. *אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁיֲהֹוָה אֱלֹהָיו*—Happy and fortunate are we, the nation (*Bnei Yisrael*) that has Hashem as our personal God Who takes care of us. *וַאֲנִי בְּחַסְדְּךָ בָטַחְתִּי*—(*Even though life is dark for me now,*) I have complete trust in Your kindness; *יָגֵל לִבִּי בִּישׁוּעָתֶךָ*—(*I am confident that in the end when all becomes clear*) my heart will rejoice because of Your salvation. *אָשִׁירָה לַיהֹוָה כִּי גָמַל עָלָי*—(*And I am so confident that it is for the good, that right now—even in my darkness*) I will sing to Hashem, because I know that all He does to me is for my good.
Serve Hashem with Simchah

he: מִזְמוֹר לְתוֹדָה
en: Mizmor LeSodah
NOTE: Even if rushed, it is best to not skip this tefillah because of its importance. Therefore, every pasuk is explained.
*Omit on Erev Pesach, Chol Hamoed Pesach, and Erev Yom Kippur.*

he: תְּהִלִּים ק
en: Tehillim 100

he: מִזְמוֹר לְתוֹדָה, הָרִיעוּ לַיהֹוָה כָּל הָאָרֶץ: עִבְדוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה בְּשִׂמְחָה, בֹּאוּ לְפָנָיו בִּרְנָנָה: דְּעוּ כִּי יְהֹוָה הוּא אֱלֹהִים, הוּא עָשָׂנוּ וְלוֹ אֲנַחְנוּ עַמּוֹ וְצֹאן מַרְעִיתוֹ: בֹּאוּ שְׁעָרָיו בְּתוֹדָה, חֲצֵרֹתָיו בִּתְהִלָּה, הוֹדוּ לוֹ בָּרְכוּ שְׁמוֹ: כִּי טוֹב יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ, וְעַד דֹּר וָדֹר אֱמוּנָתוֹ:

en: *מִזְמוֹר לְתוֹדָה*—A song of thanksgiving (*and/or, a song sung when the Korban Todah was brought in the Beis Hamikdash*): *הָרִיעוּ לַיהֹוָה*—*כָּל הָאָרֶץ* The entire earth should call out to Hashem in thanks. *עִבְדוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה בְּשִׂמְחָה*—Serve Hashem with simchah—happiness and peace of mind! *בֹּאוּ לְפָנָיו בִּרְנָנָה*—The way to get close to Him is through gladness! *דְּעוּ כִּי יְהֹוָה הוּא אֱלֹהִים*—Know that Hashem is THE One Who takes care of us (and/or, *the י-ה-ו-ה Name that represents God's attribute of mercy, is the same as אֱלֹהִים the Name that represents God's attribute of Justice—since Hashem's judgments of us are all for our benefit!*). *הוּא עָשָׂנוּ*—He created us, *וְלוֹ אֲנַחְנוּ עַמּוֹ וְצֹאן מַרְעִיתוֹ*—and we Bnei Yisrael are His unique nation, which He shepherds throughout history. *בֹּאוּ שְׁעָרָיו בְּתוֹדָה, חֲצֵרֹתָיו בִּתְהִלָּה*—We enter the Beis Hamikdash (today, we enter our mini Beis Hamikdash—our shuls and homes) with thanksgiving; *הוֹדוּ לוֹ בָּרְכוּ שְׁמוֹ*—praise Hashem for all that He does for us. *כִּי טוֹב יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ*—For Hashem is the sole absolute Good, His kindness is constant and daily in our lives (*even when we cannot see it*); *וְעַד דֹּר וָדֹר אֱמוּנָתוֹ*—and His Presence is consistent in all generations, past and future.

Why do we say Mizmor L'Sodah daily when the original Korban Todah was brought only when a person miraculously survived some life-threatening situation (like when we "bentch gomel" today)? In truth, every minute of our day is a miracle—we just don't realize it! To demand that every individual go to the Beis Hamikdash every day and offer a Korban Todah is too great a burden. But now that tefillah has taken the place of korbanos, we say Mizmor L'Sodah daily to make us appreciate the "natural" miracles we take for granted.

Since this is really about the Korban Todah, why is it not in the section of Korbanos? The answer is that this korban is very different. The Midrash tells us that when Mashiach comes, the only personal voluntary korban that will be brought will be the Korban Todah. Why? Because when Mashiach comes, it will still be a struggle for us not to take for granted all the good we have!

It is so hard to "serve Hashem with happiness and peace of mind (*בְּשִׂמְחָה*)" when our lives seem so difficult! The key—the word *בְּשִׂמְחָה* rearranged spells the word *מַחְשָׁבָה*—thinking. Happiness and peace of mind does not happen by itself. It comes from choosing to look at your life from a certain perspective. If you put your mind (*מַחְשָׁבָה*) to it—you can have peace of mind (*בְּשִׂמְחָה*)!

he: יְהִי כְבוֹד
en: Yehi Chevod

he: יְהִי כְבוֹד יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם, יִשְׂמַח יְהֹוָה בְּמַעֲשָׂיו:
יְהִי כָבוֹד
en: Hashem as Master of Nature, Hashem as Master of History
FAQ: If in the end, "only the plan of Hashem will prevail," no matter what humans think and plan, where does free free will fit in? ANSWER: Complex and deep question! See Appendix 5.

We are referred to as the עַם סְגֻלָּה—special treasure" of Hashem. The word segulah is related to the word "segol"—the three-dot triangle nekudah that appears under letters (that makes the "eh" sound). No matter which way you turn the segol, it remains the very same shape—the same segol. Similarly, Bnei Yisrael, no matter where we are or what choices we might make, we are always the unique nation of Hashem. We cannot escape that, and He will not let us go.

he: יְהִי כְבוֹד יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם, יִשְׂמַח יְהֹוָה בְּמַעֲשָׂיו: יְהִי שֵׁם יְהֹוָה מְבֹרָךְ, מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם: מִמִּזְרַח שֶׁמֶשׁ עַד מְבוֹאוֹ, מְהֻלָּל שֵׁם יְהֹוָה: רָם עַל כָּל גּוֹיִם יְהֹוָה, עַל הַשָּׁמַיִם כְּבוֹדוֹ: יְהֹוָה שִׁמְךָ לְעוֹלָם, יְהֹוָה זִכְרְךָ לְדֹר וָדֹר: יְהֹוָה בַּשָּׁמַיִם הֵכִין כִּסְאוֹ, וּמַלְכוּתוֹ בַּכֹּל מָשָׁלָה: יִשְׂמְחוּ הַשָּׁמַיִם וְתָגֵל הָאָרֶץ, וְיֹאמְרוּ בַגּוֹיִם יְהֹוָה מָלָךְ: יְהֹוָה מֶלֶךְ, יְהֹוָה מָלָךְ, יְהֹוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד: יְהֹוָה מֶלֶךְ עוֹלָם וָעֶד, אָבְדוּ גוֹיִם מֵאַרְצוֹ: יְהֹוָה הֵפִיר עֲצַת גּוֹיִם, הֵנִיא מַחְשְׁבוֹת עַמִּים: רַבּוֹת מַחֲשָׁבוֹת בְּלֶב אִישׁ, וַעֲצַת יְהֹוָה הִיא תָקוּם: עֲצַת יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם תַּעֲמֹד, מַחְשְׁבוֹת לִבּוֹ לְדֹר וָדֹר:
en: *יְהִי כְבוֹד יְהֹוָה*—May the Presence of Hashem be felt and seen clearly in the world; *(that way it will be easier for us to act appropriately)* and thus Hashem will be pleased with us—His creations. From the East to the West—the entire world, the Heavens and the earth and all the nations proclaim the glory of Hashem and His absolute and eternal rulership over "nature" and the course of world history—from the beginning of time till the end of days. *רַבּוֹת מַחֲשָׁבוֹת בְּלֶב אִישׁ*—Humans have many plans, thoughts and intentions; *וַעֲצַת יְהֹוָה הִיא תָקוּם*—but in the end, only the plan of Hashem will prevail. The "plan" Hashem has for history is eternal and spans all generations *(which is why we have difficulty grasping its entirety).*

he: כִּי בָחַר יְהֹוָה בְּצִיּוֹן, אִוָּהּ לְמוֹשָׁב לוֹ: כִּי יַעֲקֹב בָּחַר לוֹ יָהּ, יִשְׂרָאֵל לִסְגֻלָּתוֹ: כִּי לֹא יִטֹּשׁ יְהֹוָה עַמּוֹ, וְנַחֲלָתוֹ לֹא יַעֲזֹב: וְהוּא רַחוּם יְכַפֵּר עָוֹן וְלֹא יַשְׁחִית, וְהִרְבָּה לְהָשִׁיב אַפּוֹ, וְלֹא יָעִיר כָּל חֲמָתוֹ: יְהֹוָה הוֹשִׁיעָה, הַמֶּלֶךְ יַעֲנֵנוּ בְיוֹם קָרְאֵנוּ:
en: *כִּי בָחַר יְהֹוָה בְּצִיּוֹן*—Hashem has chosen Eretz Yisrael as His special land, and us, Bnei Yisrael, as His *(סְגֻלָּה)* special treasure. *כִּי לֹא יִטֹּשׁ יְהֹוָה עַמּוֹ*—and He will never destroy Bnei Yisrael or abandon His land. *וְהוּא רַחוּם יְכַפֵּר עָוֹן וְלֹא יַשְׁחִית* (Even if we mess up,) He will forgive our sins, give us another chance; *הַמֶּלֶךְ יַעֲנֵנוּ בְיוֹם* *קָרְאֵנוּ*—Hashem, please *(continue)* to save us and answer our tefillos whenever we call out to You.

he: אַשְׁרֵי / הַלְלוּיָהּ: הַלְלִי נַפְשִׁי/ כִּי טוֹב/ הַלְלוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה/ שִׁירוּ לַיהֹוָה/ הַלְלוּ יָהּ אֵל
en: Last Six Chapters of Tehillim—the Core of Pesukei D'Zimrah

he: אַשְׁרֵי
en: Ashrei

Ashrei is the core of Pesukei D'Zimrah. If one has limited time, one should at least say Ashrei. It is said three times a day (twice in Shacharis and once at Minchah). It follows the aleph-beis. The significance is that in all areas of our life (aleph to tav)—personally and nationally—we need Hashem and His help, we are supported by Him, and His providence is constant. And this is true not only when our lives seem clear and bright (Shacharis—the morning), but also when things seem to be getting dark and not so bright or clear (Minchah—as the sun sets). Because of its importance, it is explained here line by line. The remaining five chapters of Tehillim will be explained with a basic overview of each chapter.
For a set of different Instant Insights on Ashrei, please see the Ashrei of Minchah.

he: תְּהִלִּים קמה

en: The following is a preface to Tehillim 145, which begins below. These words express that it is a wonderful and joyous opportunity to praise Hashem and not, chas v'shalom, a burden!

he: אַשְׁרֵי יוֹשְׁבֵי בֵיתֶךָ, עוֹד יְהַלְלוּךָ סֶּלָה: אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁכָּכָה לּוֹ, אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁיְהֹוָה אֱלֹהָיו:
en: *אַשְׁרֵי יוֹשְׁבֵי בֵיתֶךָ*—Happy are those who *(wherever they are)* are living in the house of Hashem *(living a meaningful and spiritually growing life)*; *עוֹד יְהַלְלוּךָ סֶּלָה*—they will constantly praise You *(since they see all the circumstances of their life as opportunities for growth)*. *אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁכָּכָה לּוֹ*—Happy and fortunate are we, the nation *(Bnei Yisrael)* that has all this *(glorious history and great mission)*; *אַשְׁרֵי הָעָם שֶׁיְהֹוָה אֱלֹהָיו*—happy and fortunate are we, the nation *(Bnei Yisrael)* that has Hashem as our personal God Who takes care of us.

he: תְּהִלָּה לְדָוִד, אֲרוֹמִמְךָ אֱלוֹהַי הַמֶּלֶךְ, וַאֲבָרְכָה שִׁמְךָ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד:
en: *תְּהִלָּה לְדָוִד*—A song of praise by David HaMelech *(תְּהִלִּים קמה)*: *אֲרוֹמִמְךָ אֱלוֹהַי הַמֶּלֶךְ*—I will exalt and revere You *(since I realize that the essence of Hashem as my personal God and Ruler of the entire universe is way beyond our human comprehension)*; *וַאֲבָרְכָה שִׁמְךָ*—and *(at the same time)* I thank You *(in the following verses)* for all that You have done and will do *(for me personally and for our nation)*; *לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד*—since the beginning of time and until the end of days.

he: בְּכָל יוֹם אֲבָרְכֶךָּ, וַאֲהַלְלָה שִׁמְךָ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד:
en: *בְּכָל יוֹם אֲבָרְכֶךָּ*—(You are the Source of everything that happens to me each and every day, and) I thank You for it all *(even though some of those things are hard to understand)*; *וַאֲהַלְלָה שִׁמְךָ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד*—and in the end of days, I will truly praise You *(as all becomes clear)*.

he: גָּדוֹל יְהֹוָה וּמְהֻלָּל מְאֹד, וְלִגְדֻלָּתוֹ אֵין חֵקֶר:
en: *גָּדוֹל יְהֹוָה וּמְהֻלָּל מְאֹד*—(As much as we will thank and praise Hashem, and proclaim) Hashem is "great" and is "extremely praised"; *וְלִגְדֻלָּתוֹ אֵין חֵקֶר*—(the truth is that) the greatness of Hashem is beyond our real comprehension *("the more you know, the more you realize that you don't know")*.

he: דּוֹר לְדוֹר יְשַׁבַּח מַעֲשֶׂיךָ, וּגְבוּרֹתֶיךָ יַגִּידוּ:
en: *דּוֹר לְדוֹר יְשַׁבַּח מַעֲשֶׂיךָ*—Each generation will praise Your actions to the next generation; *וּגְבוּרֹתֶיךָ יַגִּידוּ*—and will tell them of Your *(miraculous)* mighty deeds *(that they experienced)*.

he: הֲדַר כְּבוֹד הוֹדֶךָ, וְדִבְרֵי נִפְלְאֹתֶיךָ אָשִׂיחָה:
en: *הֲדַר כְּבוֹד הוֹדֶךָ*—I will discuss *(with everyone)* the beauty of Your natural world; *וְדִבְרֵי נִפְלְאֹתֶיךָ אָשִׂיחָה*—as well as speak of Your wonders *(which are beyond our comprehension)*.

he: וֶעֱזוּז נוֹרְאֹתֶיךָ יֹאמֵרוּ, וּגְדֻלָּתְךָ אֲסַפְּרֶנָּה:
en: *וֶעֱזוּז נוֹרְאֹתֶיךָ יֹאמֵרוּ* (Says David HaMelech:) Others *(are impressed by and)* speak of Your miraculous wonders; *וּגְדֻלָּתְךָ אֲסַפְּרֶנָּה*—but I speak of Your constant and daily greatness *(which is no less miraculous)*!

he: זֵכֶר רַב טוּבְךָ יַבִּיעוּ, וְצִדְקָתְךָ יְרַנֵּנוּ:
en: *זֵכֶר רַב טוּבְךָ יַבִּיעוּ*—They *(one generation passing down the heritage to the next)* will speak of Your abundant goodness *(notwithstanding whatever difficulties that generation may have endured)*; *וְצִדְקָתְךָ יְרַנֵּנוּ*—and they sing about Your generosity *(that Hashem blesses us with more than the strict letter of the law would dictate)*.

he: חַנּוּן וְרַחוּם יְהֹוָה, אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וּגְדָל חָסֶד:
en: *חַנּוּן וְרַחוּם יְהֹוָה*—Hashem is gracious *(giving us goodness more than we deserve)* and merciful; *(and that is apparent in the fact that He is)* *אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וּגְדָל חָסֶד*—slow to anger, and thus shows His true greatness through His abundant chessed.

he: טוֹב יְהֹוָה לַכֹּל, וְרַחֲמָיו עַל כָּל מַעֲשָׂיו:
en: *טוֹב יְהֹוָה לַכֹּל*—Hashem is good to all *(of humanity—even those who have not yet lived up to their potential)*; *וְרַחֲמָיו עַל כָּל מַעֲשָׂיו*—and His mercy extends to all that He has created *(even the smallest of the animal world)*.

he: יוֹדוּךָ יְהֹוָה כָּל מַעֲשֶׂיךָ, וַחֲסִידֶיךָ יְבָרְכוּכָה:
en: *יוֹדוּךָ יְהֹוָה כָּל מַעֲשֶׂיךָ*—All of Your creations will thank You *(for Your goodness)*; *וַחֲסִידֶיךָ יְבָרְכוּכָה*—but Your righteous ones *(who have used their God-given gifts to increase sanctity and goodness into the world)* will draw increased energy from You, the Source of all blessing, and in turn *(by their good deeds)* they will increase blessing in Your world.

There is no א in Ashrei—why? The Maharal says that these pesukim, starting with the letters of the aleph-beis, express the inherent meaning *(based on Kabbalah)* of those letters. The inherent meaning of א is נְפִילָה—a falling. Here David HaMelech is describing what Hashem does, and Hashem does not cause our failings! Our mistakes are the result of our own poor choices! In the next verse we are told what Hashem does in regard to our failings *(סוֹמֵךְ יְהֹוָה לְכָל הַנֹּפְלִים)*—He helps us get back up!

he: כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתְךָ יֹאמֵרוּ, וּגְבוּרָתְךָ יְדַבֵּרוּ:
en: *כְּבוֹד מַלְכוּתְךָ יֹאמֵרוּ*—They *(Your righteous ones)* will discuss the glory of Your Kingdom *(the Hand of Hashem as seen in the beauty and complexity of the natural world—which we take for granted, and therefore must be discussed a lot)*; *וּגְבוּרָתְךָ יְדַבֵּרוּ*—and they will also tell of Your mighty open miracles *(which are clear and do not need much discussion)*.

he: לְהוֹדִיעַ לִבְנֵי הָאָדָם גְּבוּרֹתָיו, וּכְבוֹד הֲדַר מַלְכוּתוֹ:
en: *לְהוֹדִיעַ לִבְנֵי הָאָדָם גְּבוּרֹתָיו*—But when they speak to the common man, first they tell about Hashem's might and open miracles *(since that is easy for people to see)*; *וּכְבוֹד הֲדַר מַלְכוּתוֹ*—and *(from the open miracles they)* then explain to the common people how to see the Hand of Hashem in the daily miracles of "nature."

he: מַלְכוּתְךָ מַלְכוּת כָּל עֹלָמִים, וּמֶמְשַׁלְתְּךָ בְּכָל דּוֹר וָדֹר:
en: *מַלְכוּתְךָ מַלְכוּת כָּל עֹלָמִים*—Your Kingship is over all the "worlds" *(the multitude of spiritual spheres and worlds that are beyond our comprehension)*; and *וּמֶמְשַׁלְתְּךָ בְּכָל דּוֹר וָדֹר*—Your rulership spans eternity and governs each unique generation *(in its own special role in Your master plan, all of which is also beyond our human comprehension)*.

he: סוֹמֵךְ יְהֹוָה לְכָל הַנֹּפְלִים, וְזוֹקֵף לְכָל הַכְּפוּפִים:
en: *סוֹמֵךְ יְהֹוָה לְכָל הַנֹּפְלִים*—(Though Hashem is "busy" ruling over the entire universe and eternity, yet still He looks after each and every one of us constantly, and) He supports every individual who has fallen *(spiritually or emotionally)*; *וְזוֹקֵף לְכָל הַכְּפוּפִים*—and He helps every person who is bent over *(from his struggles)* to get up *(and continue his climb toward personal perfection)*.

It does not say that Hashem is close to all who call to Him with honesty and integrity. Rather, אֲשֶׁר יִקְרָאֻהוּ בֶאֱמֶת—who will call to Him with honesty, in the future tense. This means that if right now you have a very "up and down" relationship with Hashem, and you struggle with being 100 percent sincere, 100 percent of the time, and at times you are a bit of a hypocrite from what you end up doing—still, as long as you are committed to being יִקְרָאֻהוּ בֶאֱמֶת—your goal is to be completely sincere, honest, and genuine in your relationship with Hashem—then already now He will listen to your requests for help to get there!

he: עֵינֵי כֹל אֵלֶיךָ יְשַׂבֵּרוּ, וְאַתָּה נוֹתֵן לָהֶם אֶת אָכְלָם בְּעִתּוֹ:
en: *עֵינֵי כֹל אֵלֶיךָ יְשַׂבֵּרוּ*—(Therefore) every creature, from the smallest to the biggest, looks to You with hope *(that You will take care of them)*; *וְאַתָּה נוֹתֵן לָהֶם אֶת אָכְלָם בְּעִתּוֹ*—and You do give each one its *(physical, spiritual, and emotional)* sustenance—just at the time they really need it.

he: פּוֹתֵחַ אֶת יָדֶךָ, וּמַשְׂבִּיעַ לְכָל חַי רָצוֹן:
en: *פּוֹתֵחַ אֶת יָדֶךָ*—(Thus we proclaim emphatically that) You open Your Hand; *וּמַשְׂבִּיעַ לְכָל חַי רָצוֹן*—and give each person what they need in order to be satisfied. *(If you feel you are not getting what you need—focus on the next verse...)*

he: צַדִּיק יְהֹוָה בְּכָל דְּרָכָיו, וְחָסִיד בְּכָל מַעֲשָׂיו:
en: *צַדִּיק יְהֹוָה בְּכָל דְּרָכָיו*—All that Hashem does is just and righteous *(even though as humans it does not always appear that way to us)*; *וְחָסִיד בְּכָל מַעֲשָׂיו*—and He applies that justice with great kindness.

he: קָרוֹב יְהֹוָה לְכָל קֹרְאָיו, לְכֹל אֲשֶׁר יִקְרָאֻהוּ בֶאֱמֶת:
en: *קָרוֹב יְהֹוָה לְכָל קֹרְאָיו*—(In truth,) Hashem is close to all who call to Him; *לְכֹל אֲשֶׁר יִקְרָאֻהוּ* *בֶאֱמֶת*—but one must call out to Hashem with emes—honesty and sincerity. *(This means that (1) you do not say one thing in your tefillos but then by your actions distance yourself from Hashem; (2) in general, whenever you speak it is with honesty, i.e., you are a "man of your word" and sincere; and (3) it is clear from your actions that you are sincere in your requests from Hashem, since you are doing whatever you personally can do to achieve those goals.)*

he: רְצוֹן יְרֵאָיו יַעֲשֶׂה, וְאֶת שַׁוְעָתָם יִשְׁמַע וְיוֹשִׁיעֵם:

en: רְצוֹן יְרֵאָיו יַעֲשֶׂה—He fulfills the will of those who fear Him; וְאֶת שַׁוְעָתָם יִשְׁמַע וְיוֹשִׁיעֵם—and He hears their cries *(in times of trouble)* and He saves them. *(That is for those who serve Hashem from fear (מיראה).)*

he: שׁוֹמֵר יְהֹוָה אֶת כָּל אֹהֲבָיו, וְאֵת כָּל הָרְשָׁעִים יַשְׁמִיד:

en: שׁוֹמֵר יְהֹוָה אֶת כָּל אֹהֲבָיו—*(But)* those who serve Hashem from love *(מאהבה)*, He will protect them from troubles in the first place! *(Alternatively,)* for those who serve Hashem from love *(מאהבה)*, He reserves and preserves *(שומר)* their ultimate and eternal reward to be given in Olam Haba; וְאֵת כָּל הָרְשָׁעִים יַשְׁמִיד—and Hashem will destroy all evil.

he: תְּהִלַּת יְהֹוָה יְדַבֶּר פִּי, וִיבָרֵךְ כָּל בָּשָׂר שֵׁם קָדְשׁוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד:

en: תְּהִלַּת יְהֹוָה יְדַבֶּר פִּי—May my mouth declare the praises of Hashem *(so powerfully and sincerely, that)*; וִיבָרֵךְ כָּל בָּשָׂר שֵׁם קָדְשׁוֹ לְעוֹלָם וָעֶד—everyone *(will be so impacted by my words and actions, that they too)* will bless Hashem and see Him as the Source for all blessings—for eternity!
*(And to sum up that entire Chapter 145 in one sentence:)*

he: וַאֲנַחְנוּ נְבָרֵךְ יָהּ, מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם הַלְלוּיָהּ:

en: וַאֲנַחְנוּ נְבָרֵךְ יָהּ—And each of us *(in our way, based on our own life experiences,)* will thank Hashem and see Him as the Source of blessing in our individual lives; מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם—now and forever *(no matter what will happen! To sum it all up in one word:)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem!

he: תְּהִלִּים קמו

en: The focus of this chapter is on the individual—each person praises Hashem in their own way and through their own life; and Hashem's providence is on each individual in the world.

הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלִי נַפְשִׁי—My very being praises Hashem! My entire lifestyle is a Kiddush Hashem! Do not put your ultimate faith in others, that they can solve all your problems and care for all your needs. They are mere mortals whose power is limited. *(Even though we need mentors, friends, and family—no one can manage on their own, but)* real happiness and security comes from realizing that in the end, it is Hashem Who feeds *(materially and spiritually)* the hungry, and all is ultimately in His Hands.

he: הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלִי נַפְשִׁי אֶת יְהֹוָה: אֲהַלְלָה יְהֹוָה בְּחַיָּי, אֲזַמְּרָה לֵאלֹהַי בְּעוֹדִי: אַל תִּבְטְחוּ בִנְדִיבִים, בְּבֶן אָדָם שֶׁאֵין לוֹ תְשׁוּעָה: תֵּצֵא רוּחוֹ יָשֻׁב לְאַדְמָתוֹ, בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא אָבְדוּ עֶשְׁתֹּנֹתָיו: אַשְׁרֵי שֶׁאֵל יַעֲקֹב בְּעֶזְרוֹ, שִׂבְרוֹ עַל יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהָיו: עֹשֶׂה שָׁמַיִם וָאָרֶץ אֶת הַיָּם וְאֶת כָּל אֲשֶׁר בָּם, הַשֹּׁמֵר אֱמֶת לְעוֹלָם: עֹשֶׂה מִשְׁפָּט לַעֲשׁוּקִים, נֹתֵן לֶחֶם לָרְעֵבִים, יְהֹוָה מַתִּיר אֲסוּרִים: יְהֹוָה פֹּקֵחַ עִוְרִים, יְהֹוָה זֹקֵף כְּפוּפִים, יְהֹוָה אֹהֵב צַדִּיקִים: יְהֹוָה שֹׁמֵר אֶת גֵּרִים, יָתוֹם וְאַלְמָנָה יְעוֹדֵד, וְדֶרֶךְ רְשָׁעִים יְעַוֵּת: יִמְלֹךְ יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם, אֱלֹהַיִךְ צִיּוֹן לְדֹר וָדֹר, הַלְלוּיָהּ:

en: יִמְלֹךְ יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם—The Kingship of Hashem is for all eternity *(thus we can be assured that He will always be there for us, unlike our mortal helpers)*; אֱלֹהַיִךְ צִיּוֹן—and Hashem has a special providence on Eretz Yisrael; לְדֹר וָדֹר—in all generations, our past and our future; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem!

he: תְּהִלִּים קמז

en: The focus of this chapter is on Am Yisrael as a whole, and on the dawn of Era of Mashiach—when the power and providence of Hashem in our individual and national lives will finally begin to become clear.

הַלְלוּיָהּ, כִּי טוֹב זַמְּרָה—As the Era of Mashiach dawns, and all confusion begins to clear, it will be pleasant and enjoyable to sing out and praise Hashem! Hashem **is** the builder of Yerushalayim, and He will gather in all the exiles, and heal all the broken bodies and souls. Hashem, Who controls the vastness of nature, views each individual Jew like a star, and each one is beloved by Hashem. Thus everyone will sing praises to Hashem. *(Unlike now, in the Era of Mashiach)* the borders of Eretz Yisrael will be secure, the gates of Yerushalayim will be strong—we will realize that our true ultimate protection is from Hashem.

לֹא עָשָׂה כֵן לְכָל גּוֹי—Such unique providence and miraculous continuity was not shown to any other nation; וּמִשְׁפָּטִים בַּל יְדָעוּם—and only we are entrusted with His Torah and Divine laws *(and therefore)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem!

הַלְלוּיָהּ, כִּי טוֹב זַמְּרָה literally translates as, "Hashem is building Yerushalayim"—present tense—even though in context it is referring to the Era of Mashiach. That is telling us that in truth, every single thing that happened and happens to us as individuals and as a nation is part of the process needed to build Yerushalayim. Thus all through our galus, all that Hashem has sent our way is part of a continuous building of Yerushalayim. Once Mashiach comes, that will all be clear.

he: הַלְלוּיָהּ, כִּי טוֹב זַמְּרָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ, כִּי נָעִים נָאוָה תְהִלָּה: בּוֹנֵה יְרוּשָׁלַיִם יְהֹוָה, נִדְחֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל יְכַנֵּס: הָרֹפֵא לִשְׁבוּרֵי לֵב, וּמְחַבֵּשׁ לְעַצְּבוֹתָם: מוֹנֶה מִסְפָּר לַכּוֹכָבִים, לְכֻלָּם שֵׁמוֹת יִקְרָא: גָּדוֹל אֲדוֹנֵינוּ וְרַב כֹּחַ, לִתְבוּנָתוֹ אֵין מִסְפָּר: מְעוֹדֵד עֲנָוִים יְהֹוָה, מַשְׁפִּיל רְשָׁעִים עֲדֵי אָרֶץ: עֱנוּ לַיהֹוָה בְּתוֹדָה, זַמְּרוּ לֵאלֹהֵינוּ בְכִנּוֹר: הַמְכַסֶּה שָׁמַיִם בְּעָבִים, הַמֵּכִין לָאָרֶץ מָטָר, הַמַּצְמִיחַ הָרִים חָצִיר: נוֹתֵן לִבְהֵמָה לַחְמָהּ, לִבְנֵי עֹרֵב אֲשֶׁר יִקְרָאוּ: לֹא בִגְבוּרַת הַסּוּס יֶחְפָּץ, לֹא בְשׁוֹקֵי הָאִישׁ יִרְצֶה: רוֹצֶה יְהֹוָה אֶת יְרֵאָיו, אֶת הַמְיַחֲלִים לְחַסְדּוֹ: שַׁבְּחִי יְרוּשָׁלַיִם אֶת יְהֹוָה, הַלְלִי אֱלֹהַיִךְ צִיּוֹן: כִּי חִזַּק בְּרִיחֵי שְׁעָרָיִךְ, בֵּרַךְ בָּנַיִךְ בְּקִרְבֵּךְ: הַשָּׂם גְּבוּלֵךְ שָׁלוֹם, חֵלֶב חִטִּים יַשְׂבִּיעֵךְ: הַשֹּׁלֵחַ אִמְרָתוֹ אָרֶץ, עַד מְהֵרָה יָרוּץ דְּבָרוֹ: הַנֹּתֵן שֶׁלֶג כַּצָּמֶר, כְּפוֹר כָּאֵפֶר יְפַזֵּר: מַשְׁלִיךְ קַרְחוֹ כְפִתִּים, לִפְנֵי קָרָתוֹ מִי יַעֲמֹד: יִשְׁלַח דְּבָרוֹ וְיַמְסֵם, יַשֵּׁב רוּחוֹ יִזְּלוּ מָיִם: מַגִּיד דְּבָרָו לְיַעֲקֹב, חֻקָּיו וּמִשְׁפָּטָיו לְיִשְׂרָאֵל: לֹא עָשָׂה כֵן לְכָל גּוֹי, וּמִשְׁפָּטִים בַּל יְדָעוּם, הַלְלוּיָהּ:
תְּהִלִּים קמח

en: The focus of this chapter is on the Era of Mashiach itself and how the entire spiritual and natural world, as well as all the nations of the world, will all sing the praises of Hashem as they recognize the unique providence of Hashem on His people.

הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה מִן הַשָּׁמַיִם—In the Era of Mashiach, it will be evident to all that the Heavens and angels; the sun, moon, and stars; the great mountains, forests, oceans, and the entire animal kingdom—all praise Hashem! In addition, all the nations of the world—the rulers and the regular people—will recognize the power and providence of Hashem on nature and on us, His people.

he: הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה מִן הַשָּׁמַיִם, הַלְלוּהוּ בַּמְּרוֹמִים: הַלְלוּהוּ כָל מַלְאָכָיו, הַלְלוּהוּ כָּל צְבָאָיו: הַלְלוּהוּ שֶׁמֶשׁ וְיָרֵחַ, הַלְלוּהוּ כָּל כּוֹכְבֵי אוֹר: הַלְלוּהוּ שְׁמֵי הַשָּׁמָיִם, וְהַמַּיִם אֲשֶׁר מֵעַל הַשָּׁמָיִם: יְהַלְלוּ אֶת שֵׁם יְהֹוָה, כִּי הוּא צִוָּה וְנִבְרָאוּ: וַיַּעֲמִידֵם לָעַד לְעוֹלָם, חָק נָתַן וְלֹא יַעֲבוֹר: הַלְלוּ אֶת יְהֹוָה מִן הָאָרֶץ, תַּנִּינִים וְכָל תְּהֹמוֹת: אֵשׁ וּבָרָד שֶׁלֶג וְקִיטוֹר, רוּחַ סְעָרָה עֹשָׂה דְבָרוֹ: הֶהָרִים וְכָל גְּבָעוֹת, עֵץ פְּרִי וְכָל אֲרָזִים: הַחַיָּה וְכָל בְּהֵמָה, רֶמֶשׂ וְצִפּוֹר כָּנָף: מַלְכֵי אֶרֶץ וְכָל לְאֻמִּים, שָׂרִים וְכָל שֹׁפְטֵי אָרֶץ: בַּחוּרִים וְגַם בְּתוּלוֹת, זְקֵנִים עִם נְעָרִים: יְהַלְלוּ אֶת שֵׁם יְהֹוָה, כִּי נִשְׂגָּב שְׁמוֹ לְבַדּוֹ, הוֹדוֹ עַל אֶרֶץ וְשָׁמָיִם: וַיָּרֶם קֶרֶן לְעַמּוֹ, תְּהִלָּה לְכָל חֲסִידָיו, לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל עַם קְרֹבוֹ, הַלְלוּיָהּ:

en: וַיָּרֶם קֶרֶן לְעַמּוֹ—*(And how will all this come about? Because)* Hashem will raise the glory of His nation *(such that)*; תְּהִלָּה לְכָל חֲסִידָיו—everyone will praise His righteous ones *(and who are His righteous ones?)*; לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל עַם קְרֹבוֹ—us, Bnei Yisrael *(including me!)* who stayed close to Hashem *(when it was not easy and clear!)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—*(and therefore,)* whatever happens, praise Hashem!

he: תְּהִלִּים קמט

en: The focus of this chapter is on the next stage—the new era when the entire world will join with Bnei Yisrael to sing praises to Hashem—and submit to His Will.

הַלְלוּיָהּ, שִׁירוּ לַיהֹוָה שִׁיר חָדָשׁ—All will sing a new song of praise to Hashem; תְּהִלָּתוֹ בִּקְהַל חֲסִידִים—He will be praised by all righteous peoples. *(But even in that new era)* we, Bnei Yisrael, will be especially joyous, since Hashem has always been our King, and made us His unique nation *(long before Mashiach's arrival)*. And Hashem has special favor for us since we showed our allegiance to His Torah *(even when life was difficult and not clear)*!

לַעֲשׂוֹת בָּהֶם מִשְׁפָּט כָּתוּב—And in that era, Hashem will mete out the final punishments to all those nations who did evil, as is clearly foretold in the Torah; הָדָר הוּא לְכָל חֲסִידָיו—and bring everlasting glory to His righteous ones. *(And therefore)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem!

he: הַלְלוּיָהּ, שִׁירוּ לַיהֹוָה שִׁיר חָדָשׁ, תְּהִלָּתוֹ בִּקְהַל חֲסִידִים: יִשְׂמַח יִשְׂרָאֵל בְּעֹשָׂיו, בְּנֵי צִיּוֹן יָגִילוּ בְמַלְכָּם: יְהַלְלוּ שְׁמוֹ בְמָחוֹל, בְּתֹף וְכִנּוֹר יְזַמְּרוּ לוֹ: כִּי רוֹצֶה יְהֹוָה בְּעַמּוֹ, יְפָאֵר עֲנָוִים בִּישׁוּעָה: יַעְלְזוּ חֲסִידִים בְּכָבוֹד, יְרַנְּנוּ עַל מִשְׁכְּבוֹתָם: רוֹמְמוֹת אֵל בִּגְרוֹנָם, וְחֶרֶב פִּיפִיּוֹת בְּיָדָם: לַעֲשׂוֹת נְקָמָה בַּגּוֹיִם, תּוֹכֵחוֹת בַּלְאֻמִּים: לֶאְסֹר מַלְכֵיהֶם בְּזִקִּים, וְנִכְבְּדֵיהֶם בְּכַבְלֵי בַרְזֶל: לַעֲשׂוֹת בָּהֶם מִשְׁפָּט כָּתוּב, הָדָר הוּא לְכָל חֲסִידָיו, הַלְלוּיָהּ:
תְּהִלִּים קנ

en: This chapter, the very last chapter of the book of Tehillim, sums up the previous chapters.

הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלוּ אֵל בְּקָדְשׁוֹ—We proclaim the greatness of Hashem as seen by His acts that are above nature, and as seen by His providence within nature. We praise Hashem with every sort of musical instrument *(meaning—with all our sometimes-conflicting thoughts, emotions, and no matter what mood we are in—at all times and in all circumstances—we sing out to Hashem, knowing that all is for our best)*.

כֹּל הַנְּשָׁמָה תְּהַלֵּל יָהּ—Every neshamah—every individual—should praise Hashem; our entire complex neshamah should, as one harmonious whole, praise Hashem; every breath we take *(all that we do)* should bring praise to Hashem. *(And therefore)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem!

he: הַלְלוּיָהּ, הַלְלוּ אֵל בְּקָדְשׁוֹ, הַלְלוּהוּ בִּרְקִיעַ עֻזּוֹ: הַלְלוּהוּ בִגְבוּרֹתָיו, הַלְלוּהוּ כְּרֹב גֻּדְלוֹ: הַלְלוּהוּ בְּתֵקַע שׁוֹפָר, הַלְלוּהוּ בְּנֵבֶל וְכִנּוֹר: הַלְלוּהוּ בְּתֹף וּמָחוֹל, הַלְלוּהוּ בְּמִנִּים וְעֻגָב: הַלְלוּהוּ בְצִלְצְלֵי שָׁמַע, הַלְלוּהוּ בְּצִלְצְלֵי תְרוּעָה: כֹּל הַנְּשָׁמָה תְּהַלֵּל יָהּ, הַלְלוּיָהּ: כֹּל הַנְּשָׁמָה תְּהַלֵּל יָהּ, הַלְלוּיָהּ:
בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם
en: And to Sum Up Pesukei D'Zimrah

בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם אָמֵן וְאָמֵן—I am absolutely certain that Hashem is the Source of all that was and all that will be. שֹׁכֵן יְרוּשָׁלָיִם—And that Hashem is the Source of our eternal connection to Eretz Yisrael and Yerushalayim. *(And therefore)*; הַלְלוּיָהּ—whatever happens, praise Hashem! בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהִים אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל—Hashem, the Source of everything, Who has taken care of us, Bnei Yisrael; עֹשֵׂה נִפְלָאוֹת לְבַדּוֹ—He alone performs wonderous miracles!

he: בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה לְעוֹלָם אָמֵן וְאָמֵן: בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה מִצִּיּוֹן, שֹׁכֵן יְרוּשָׁלָיִם הַלְלוּיָהּ: בָּרוּךְ יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהִים אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל, עֹשֵׂה נִפְלָאוֹת לְבַדּוֹ: וּבָרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹדוֹ לְעוֹלָם, וְיִמָּלֵא כְבוֹדוֹ אֶת כָּל הָאָרֶץ, אָמֵן וְאָמֵן:

en: וּבָרוּךְ שֵׁם כְּבוֹדוֹ לְעוֹלָם—And may the perception of Hashem's Presence be increased forever; וְיִמָּלֵא כְבוֹדוֹ אֶת כָּל הָאָרֶץ—and may the perception of Hashem's Presence be felt by everyone, over the entire earth. אָמֵן וְאָמֵן—This is the absolute truth, and I hope it will happen soon!

he: וַיְבָרֶךְ דָּוִד / וּכְרוֹת עִמּוֹ הַבְּרִית
en: Introduction to Shiras Ha'yam

Before we start asking Hashem for the stuff (we think) we need (in Shemoneh Esrei), we say Pesukei D'Zimrah to thank Him for what we **do** have, and to emphasize our awareness and belief that it is Hashem alone Who controls our circumstances. But why do we want all this stuff in the first place? The answer should be, because we want all the tools possible to fulfill our part of this great mission to the world called Am Yisrael!

Therefore, the climax of Pesukei D'Zimrah will be the Shiras Ha'yam—the praise Bnei Yisrael sang when Hashem clearly showed us and the world that we are His special nation with a special mission. This Shiras Ha'yam is introduced with the following two paragraphs. The first (וַיְבָרֶךְ דָּוִד) is David Hamelech speaking to the entire nation at the end of his life. He handed over to his son Shlomo all that he had collected for the building of the Beis Hamikdash (which he knew he would not live to see) and looks back at all he went through and expresses his hopes for the future. The second (אַתָּה הוּא) was said by the Navi Nechemiah when that Beis Hamikdash lay in ruins! Now Bnei Yisrael are being urged to come back and make the sacrifices necessary to rebuild the Beis Hamikdash in particular, and the overall glory of our mission.

The message: Whether we feel we have worked hard, accomplished much, and have great hopes for the future, or we feel that all has come crashing down and we are looking for the energy to climb back up—we simply praise Hashem for what we do have. Most importantly, we focus on the fact that we just need to do the best we can, and the final outcome is in the Hands of Hashem.
FAQ: Well, to be honest, I just want all this stuff because—I want it! Is that so bad? ANSWER: Honest question! See Appendix B.
Stand until after Barchu

וַיְבָרֶךְ דָּוִד—David HaMelech proclaims publicly: Hashem—You are the Source of everything *(that I have achieved)*—all greatness, power, wealth, and rulership is Yours, Hashem. You have the power to give us the strength *(to become great and fulfill our mission)*. And we thank You for all You have done!

The first letters of the opening four words—חֲבָר"ך—spell חבר—"חבר," "חַבָּר," one of the Names of Hashem, which represents His attribute of certainty and reliability. Notwithstanding all the difficulties in his life, David HaMelech always lived with the belief that Hashem can be relied on to take care of him.

he: וַיְבָרֶךְ דָּוִד אֶת יְהֹוָה לְעֵינֵי כָּל הַקָּהָל, וַיֹּאמֶר דָּוִד בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל אָבִינוּ, מֵעוֹלָם וְעַד עוֹלָם: לְךָ יְהֹוָה הַגְּדֻלָּה, וְהַגְּבוּרָה, וְהַתִּפְאֶרֶת וְהַנֵּצַח וְהַהוֹד, כִּי כֹל בַּשָּׁמַיִם וּבָאָרֶץ, לְךָ יְהֹוָה הַמַּמְלָכָה וְהַמִּתְנַשֵּׂא לְכֹל לְרֹאשׁ:
נוֹהֲגִים לִכְרוֹעַ צֶדֶק)
en: (it is customary to bow)

he: וְהָעֹשֶׁר וְהַכָּבוֹד מִלְּפָנֶיךָ, וְאַתָּה מוֹשֵׁל בַּכֹּל, וּבְיָדְךָ כֹּחַ וּגְבוּרָה, וּבְיָדְךָ לְגַדֵּל וּלְחַזֵּק לַכֹּל: וְעַתָּה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מוֹדִים אֲנַחְנוּ לָךְ, וּמְהַלְלִים לְשֵׁם תִּפְאַרְתֶּךָ:

en: אַתָּה הוּא—The Navi Nechemiah proclaims: You, Hashem, alone, created the entire universe, and You chose Avraham! You saved him from Ur Kasdim;

וְכָרוֹת הַבְּרִית—and You established an everlasting covenant to give Eretz Yisrael to his descendants *(that's us!)*. You saw the suffering of Bnei Yisrael in Egypt, and You performed great public miracles—culminating with splitting the sea in order to save us, Your nation *(for a great mission to the world)*.

he: אַתָּה הוּא יְהֹוָה לְבַדֶּךָ, אַתָּה עָשִׂיתָ אֶת הַשָּׁמַיִם שְׁמֵי הַשָּׁמַיִם וְכָל צְבָאָם, הָאָרֶץ וְכָל אֲשֶׁר עָלֶיהָ, הַיַּמִּים וְכָל אֲשֶׁר בָּהֶם, וְאַתָּה מְחַיֶּה אֶת כֻּלָּם, וּצְבָא הַשָּׁמַיִם לְךָ מִשְׁתַּחֲוִים: אַתָּה הוּא יְהֹוָה הָאֱלֹהִים, אֲשֶׁר בָּחַרְתָּ בְּאַבְרָם, וְהוֹצֵאתוֹ מֵאוּר כַּשְׂדִּים, וְשַׂמְתָּ שְּׁמוֹ אַבְרָהָם: וּמָצָאתָ אֶת לְבָבוֹ נֶאֱמָן לְפָנֶיךָ:

וְכָרוֹת עִמּוֹ הַבְּרִית לָתֵת אֶת אֶרֶץ הַכְּנַעֲנִי הַחִתִּי הָאֱמֹרִי וְהַפְּרִזִּי וְהַיְבוּסִי וְהַגִּרְגָּשִׁי לָתֵת לְזַרְעוֹ, וַתָּקֶם אֶת דְּבָרֶיךָ כִּי צַדִּיק אָתָּה: וַתֵּרֶא אֶת עֳנִי אֲבֹתֵינוּ בְּמִצְרָיִם, וְאֶת זַעֲקָתָם שָׁמַעְתָּ עַל יַם סוּף: וַתִּתֵּן אֹתֹת וּמֹפְתִים בְּפַרְעֹה וּבְכָל עֲבָדָיו וּבְכָל עַם אַרְצוֹ, כִּי יָדַעְתָּ כִּי הֵזִידוּ עֲלֵיהֶם, וַתַּעַשׂ לְךָ שֵׁם כְּהַיּוֹם הַזֶּה: וְהַיָּם בָּקַעְתָּ לִפְנֵיהֶם, וַיַּעַבְרוּ בְתוֹךְ הַיָּם בַּיַּבָּשָׁה, וְאֶת רֹדְפֵיהֶם הִשְׁלַכְתָּ בִמְצוֹלֹת כְּמוֹ אֶבֶן בְּמַיִם עַזִּים:
וַיּוֹשַׁע יְהֹוָה בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא / אָז יָשִׁיר
en: Shiras Ha'yam—Conclusion of Pesukei D'Zimrah

As the Torah tells us: וַיּוֹשַׁע—On that day Hashem rescued Bnei Yisrael from Egypt, and Bnei Yisrael personally saw all the Egyptians dead on the seashore. Bnei Yisrael saw the great power that Hashem unleashed against Egypt; the people were in awe of Hashem; they trusted Hashem and believed in His servant Moshe.

he: וַיּוֹשַׁע יְהֹוָה בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא אֶת יִשְׂרָאֵל מִיַּד מִצְרָיִם, וַיַּרְא יִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת מִצְרַיִם מֵת עַל שְׂפַת הַיָּם: וַיַּרְא יִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת הַיָּד הַגְּדֹלָה אֲשֶׁר עָשָׂה יְהֹוָה בְּמִצְרַיִם וַיִּירְאוּ הָעָם אֶת יְהֹוָה, וַיַּאֲמִינוּ בַּיהֹוָה וּבְמֹשֶׁה עַבְדּוֹ:

en: אָז יָשִׁיר מֹשֶׁה—Then Moshe and Bnei Yisrael chose to sing this Shirah to Hashem and said: I will sing to Hashem for He has triumphed over the haughty *(Egyptians)*; His strength and power saved me. This is my God *(I will create my own personal relationship with Hashem)*, and He is the God of my father *(and that unique relationship will be built on the foundations of our ancestors)*. Hashem cast Pharaoh's chariots and army into the sea and they all drowned; at the same time the flowing waters stood like walls *(to save us)*. The enemy said, "I will chase them, overtake them, and obliterate them!" You made the wind blow, and the sea covered them, and they sank like lead!

מִי כָמֹכָה בָּאֵלִם יְהֹוָה—Among all the powers *(powers of nature, powers of humans)*, who is equal to You, Hashem?; מִי כָּמֹכָה נֶאְדָּר בַּקֹּדֶשׁ—Who is like You, powerful but with purity of purpose?; נוֹרָא תְהִלֹּת עֹשֵׂה פֶלֶא—Our songs praising You instill in us awe of Your wondrous deeds, which are in truth beyond our comprehension. All the nations of the world shuddered with fear *(when they heard of this great miracle)*. May dread and fear fall upon them until Your nation crosses over *(into Eretz Yisrael)*, the place Your Presence is most felt.

he: אָז יָשִׁיר מֹשֶׁה וּבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת הַשִּׁירָה הַזֹּאת לַיהֹוָה, וַיֹּאמְרוּ לֵאמֹר, אָשִׁירָה לַיהֹוָה כִּי גָאֹה גָּאָה, סוּס וְרֹכְבוֹ רָמָה בַיָּם: עָזִּי וְזִמְרָת יָהּ וַיְהִי לִי לִישׁוּעָה, זֶה אֵלִי וְאַנְוֵהוּ, אֱלֹהֵי אָבִי וַאֲרֹמְמֶנְהוּ: יְהֹוָה אִישׁ מִלְחָמָה, יְהֹוָה שְׁמוֹ: מַרְכְּבֹת פַּרְעֹה וְחֵילוֹ יָרָה בַיָּם, וּמִבְחַר שָׁלִשָׁיו טֻבְּעוּ בְיַם סוּף: תְּהֹמֹת יְכַסְיֻמוּ, יָרְדוּ בִמְצוֹלֹת כְּמוֹ אָבֶן: יְמִינְךָ יְהֹוָה נֶאְדָּרִי בַּכֹּחַ, יְמִינְךָ יְהֹוָה תִּרְעַץ אוֹיֵב: וּבְרֹב גְּאוֹנְךָ תַּהֲרֹס קָמֶיךָ, תְּשַׁלַּח חֲרֹנְךָ יֹאכְלֵמוֹ כַּקַּשׁ: וּבְרוּחַ אַפֶּיךָ נֶעֶרְמוּ מַיִם, נִצְּבוּ כְמוֹ נֵד נֹזְלִים, קָפְאוּ תְהֹמֹת בְּלֶב יָם: אָמַר אוֹיֵב אֶרְדֹּף אַשִּׂיג אֲחַלֵּק שָׁלָל תִּמְלָאֵמוֹ נַפְשִׁי, אָרִיק חַרְבִּי תּוֹרִישֵׁמוֹ יָדִי: נָשַׁפְתָּ בְרוּחֲךָ כִּסָּמוֹ יָם, צָלֲלוּ כַּעוֹפֶרֶת בְּמַיִם אַדִּירִים: מִי כָמֹכָה בָּאֵלִם יְהֹוָה, מִי כָּמֹכָה נֶאְדָּר בַּקֹּדֶשׁ, נוֹרָא תְהִלֹּת עֹשֵׂה פֶלֶא: נָטִיתָ יְמִינְךָ תִּבְלָעֵמוֹ אָרֶץ: נָחִיתָ בְחַסְדְּךָ עַם זוּ גָּאָלְתָּ, נֵהַלְתָּ בְעָזְּךָ אֶל נְוֵה קָדְשֶׁךָ: שָׁמְעוּ עַמִּים יִרְגָּזוּן, חִיל אָחַז יֹשְׁבֵי פְּלָשֶׁת:

en: יְהֹוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד—*Just as we have seen the rulership of Hashem here at the sea, so too for all future times)* Hashem will reign forever and ever!

כִּי לַיהֹוָה הַמְּלוּכָה וּמֹשֵׁל בַּגּוֹיִם—*Once Mashiach comes)* the entire world will recognize Hashem as the King of His nation, Bnei Yisrael, and the ruler over all nations. On that day, Hashem will be recognized as the sole power, and His Presence will be felt by all.

he: אָז נִבְהֲלוּ אַלּוּפֵי אֱדוֹם, אֵילֵי מוֹאָב יֹאחֲזֵמוֹ רָעַד, נָמֹגוּ כֹּל יֹשְׁבֵי כְנָעַן: תִּפֹּל עֲלֵיהֶם אֵימָתָה וָפַחַד, בִּגְדֹל זְרוֹעֲךָ יִדְּמוּ כָּאָבֶן, עַד יַעֲבֹר עַמְּךָ יְהֹוָה, עַד יַעֲבֹר עַם זוּ קָנִיתָ: תְּבִאֵמוֹ וְתִטָּעֵמוֹ בְּהַר נַחֲלָתְךָ, מָכוֹן לְשִׁבְתְּךָ פָּעַלְתָּ יְהֹוָה, מִקְּדָשׁ אֲדֹנָי כּוֹנֲנוּ יָדֶיךָ: יְהֹוָה יִמְלֹךְ לְעֹלָם וָעֶד: יְהֹוָה מַלְכוּתֵהּ קָאֵם לְעָלַם וּלְעָלְמֵי עָלְמַיָּא: כִּי בָא סוּס פַּרְעֹה בְּרִכְבּוֹ וּבְפָרָשָׁיו בַּיָּם, וַיָּשֶׁב יְהֹוָה עֲלֵהֶם אֶת מֵי הַיָּם, וּבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל הָלְכוּ בַיַּבָּשָׁה בְּתוֹךְ הַיָּם: כִּי לַיהֹוָה הַמְּלוּכָה, וּמֹשֵׁל בַּגּוֹיִם: וְעָלוּ מוֹשִׁיעִים בְּהַר צִיּוֹן לִשְׁפֹּט אֶת הַר עֵשָׂו, וְהָיְתָה לַיהֹוָה הַמְּלוּכָה: וְהָיָה יְהֹוָה לְמֶלֶךְ עַל כָּל הָאָרֶץ, בַּיּוֹם הַהוּא יִהְיֶה יְהֹוָה אֶחָד וּשְׁמוֹ אֶחָד:
יִשְׁתַּבַּח
en: Concluding Brachah of Pesukei D'Zimrah
We do not fully realize all the good You do for us; nor do we have the ability to adequately thank and praise You. Thus we end Pesukei D'Zimrah by saying simply:

יִשְׁתַּבַּח שִׁמְךָ לָעַד מַלְכֵּנוּ—Hashem our King, Who is קָדוֹשׁ *(above all rules of nature)* and is King of the entire Heavens and earth, May the impact of all You do *(שֶׁבַר)* be praised forever. For specifically to You, Hashem, Who controls everything and has taken care of Bnei Yisrael throughout history, is it fitting to offer all praises and thanksgiving, now and forever.

he: יִשְׁתַּבַּח שִׁמְךָ לָעַד מַלְכֵּנוּ, הָאֵל הַמֶּלֶךְ הַגָּדוֹל וְהַקָּדוֹשׁ בַּשָּׁמַיִם וּבָאָרֶץ, כִּי לְךָ נָאֶה, יְהֹוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ וֵאלֹהֵי אֲבוֹתֵינוּ, שִׁיר וּשְׁבָחָה, הַלֵּל וְזִמְרָה, עֹז וּמֶמְשָׁלָה, נֶצַח, גְּדֻלָּה וּגְבוּרָה, תְּהִלָּה וְתִפְאֶרֶת, קְדֻשָּׁה וּמַלְכוּת, בְּרָכוֹת וְהוֹדָאוֹת, מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם:

en: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה—You, Hashem, are the Source of everything; אֵל מֶלֶךְ גָּדוֹל בַּתִּשְׁבָּחוֹת—all-powerful King Who is greater than any praises *(that we mere humans can say)*; אֵל הַהוֹדָאוֹת—to Whom all thanksgiving is due; אֲדוֹן הַנִּפְלָאוֹת—Master of wonders; הַבּוֹחֵר בְּשִׁירֵי זִמְרָה—Who takes pleasure in songs of praise; מֶלֶךְ אֵל חֵי הָעוֹלָמִים—You are the King Who gives life to the world.

FAQ: What does it mean that Hashem takes pleasure in songs of praise? Is the בּוֹחֵר בְּשִׁירֵי takes pleasure—Hashem is not human; what does it mean that He takes pleasure? ANSWER: Good question! See Appendix 10.

he: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהֹוָה, אֵל מֶלֶךְ גָּדוֹל בַּתִּשְׁבָּחוֹת, אֵל הַהוֹדָאוֹת, אֲדוֹן הַנִּפְלָאוֹת, הַבּוֹחֵר בְּשִׁירֵי זִמְרָה, מֶלֶךְ אֵל חֵי הָעוֹלָמִים:
תְּהִלִּים קל
en: During Aseres Yemei Teshuvah, some add

The focus of this chapter is that even when we are מִמַּעֲמַקִּים—*(note that it is plural, meaning "depths")* experiencing either the depths of misfortune and difficulties or the depths of feelings of guilt because we know we have messed up; no matter what, we can turn to Hashem because He is full of chessed, and the הַרְבֵּה עִמּוֹ פְדוּת—His power to redeem and forgive us is great.

he: שִׁיר הַמַּעֲלוֹת, מִמַּעֲמַקִּים קְרָאתִיךָ יְהֹוָה: אֲדֹנָי שִׁמְעָה בְקוֹלִי, תִּהְיֶינָה אָזְנֶיךָ קַשֻּׁבוֹת לְקוֹל תַּחֲנוּנָי: אִם עֲוֹנוֹת תִּשְׁמָר יָהּ, אֲדֹנָי מִי יַעֲמֹד: כִּי עִמְּךָ הַסְּלִיחָה, לְמַעַן תִּוָּרֵא: קִוִּיתִי יְהֹוָה קִוְּתָה נַפְשִׁי, וְלִדְבָרוֹ הוֹחָלְתִּי: נַפְשִׁי לַאדֹנָי, מִשֹּׁמְרִים לַבֹּקֶר, שֹׁמְרִים לַבֹּקֶר: יַחֵל יִשְׂרָאֵל אֶל יְהֹוָה, כִּי עִם יְהֹוָה הַחֶסֶד, וְהַרְבֵּה עִמּוֹ פְדוּת: וְהוּא יִפְדֶּה אֶת יִשְׂרָאֵל, מִכֹּל עֲוֹנֹתָיו:

en: שִׁיר הַמַּעֲלוֹת, מִמַּעֲמַקִּים—I know that I have not lived up to my potential, and I accept that if You would judge me by the strict letter of law, I could not survive! But I do want to create a relationship with You. And thus I turn to You, Hashem, for You are the Source of loving-kindness and forgiveness, and ask that You give me another chance to prove myself.
